title: 3 planets will put on a sky show in May — when and how to see them
summary: Venus, Jupiter, and Mercury will align in the sky in May, offering a rare three-planet display. The event will be visible with stunning moon pairings, providing a spectacular skywatching opportunity.

title: The Eta Aquarid meteor shower peaks next week: Here's what you need to know
summary: The Eta Aquarid meteor shower is set to peak next week, with shooting stars and occasional fireballs visible when the constellation Aquarius is above the horizon.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts get the star treatment after historic moon trip
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts received significant media attention following their historic moon mission announcement. They were prominently featured on major networks despite not having a traditional parade.

title: Drone radar could help spacecraft pinpoint where to drill for water on Mars, scientists say
summary: Scientists propose using drone-mounted radar to map buried ice on Mars, aiding future spacecraft in selecting drilling sites. The technology was successfully tested on Earth's glaciers.

title: 'Slither' at 20: The alien worm comedy-horror that heralded James Gunn's arrival
summary: The article discusses the 2006 alien worm comedy-horror film 'Slither,' which marked James Gunn's directorial debut. Despite low theater attendance, the movie established Gunn as a filmmaker worth watching.

title: How 'For All Mankind' evolved from 'The Right Stuff' into 'The Expanse'
summary: The TV series 'For All Mankind' evolved from a period piece akin to 'The Right Stuff' into a hard science fiction narrative akin to 'The Expanse'. This transformation reflects a shift toward more scientifically accurate space exploration storytelling.

title: £115 off for Star Wars Day, I think this Lego Star Wars UCS set is the most mind-blowing Lego starship you'll ever own
summary: Argos UK is offering a 20% discount on the Lego Star Wars Clone Wars Venator starship for Star Wars Day. The massive 5,000+ piece UCS set is highlighted as one of the best Lego starship models available.

title: Thinner than a hair and stretchy like rubber: New material could shield against radiation in next-gen space tech
summary: Scientists have created a thin, stretchy material that could protect against radiation in future space technology. This innovation may enhance safety for humans and critical systems during space missions.

title: Is it cake? No, it's a parachute! | Space photo of the day for May 1, 2026
summary: A donut-shaped bag containing a massive parachute is featured in a space photo for May 1, 2026, hinting at its use for a Mars mission.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight test, featuring a revised trajectory. The return of Starship launches is expected within weeks.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A doctor with experience in mass casualty events and overseas service has been selected as the next NASA astronaut for an upcoming ISS mission. The astronaut discussed preparations and goals for the mission.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center, following the successful Artemis II lunar flyby mission. This marks a key step toward the next crewed Moon landing.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy, ULA's Atlas V, and Arianespace's Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's LEO satellites this week from multiple locations. The launches will take place in Florida, California, and French Guiana.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic compounds on Mars, providing fresh clues about ancient life on the Red Planet. The findings mark a significant advancement in the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur Cosmodrome to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS). This mission follows the CRS NG-24 cargo launch to the ISS two weeks prior.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the first flight of Block 3 Starship at the Cape, aiming to launch this year. The development marks a significant milestone for the company's next-generation spacecraft.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the timeline and progress of developing new spacesuits for future missions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication commemorates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test, likely related to a spacecraft landing system, was conducted. The 'bake-out' suggests a thermal test to ensure the parachute's performance under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to avoid microbial contamination, ensuring it meets planetary protection standards. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will help slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing space debris or satellites. This innovation aims to improve space operations and safety.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session in April-May and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received at an optimal location. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the project.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has captured 57 artificial solar eclipses and over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona videos since July 2025. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously thought.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Space Nuclear Execs Cheer the FY27 Budget Proposal
summary: NASA's FY27 budget proposal has been praised by space nuclear executives, with Administrator Isaacman highlighting it as a logical evolution for the agency. The proposal signals strong support for advancing space nuclear technology.

title: House Subcommittee Approves $24.4B NASA Budget
summary: The House Subcommittee has approved a $24.4B budget for NASA, rejecting the administration's FY27 request. This marks a legislative step to formalize the disagreement with the proposed spending plan.

title: European Space Firms Raised €1.4B of Private Capital in 2025
summary: European space firms secured €1.4 billion in private funding in 2025, indicating growing investor confidence in the sector. This trend highlights the expanding role of private capital in supporting space industry startups.

title: BioOrbit Raises £9.8M Seed To Manufacture Cancer Drugs in Orbit
summary: BioOrbit has raised £9.8 million in seed funding to manufacture cancer drugs in orbit using microgravity. The company aims to reformulate drugs for self-administration by patients.

title: CATALYST Unveils its Sat Product for City Skylines
summary: CATALYST has introduced a new satellite product designed for urban planning and risk assessment. The product aims to provide construction firms, insurers, and city authorities with pre-construction insights and monitoring capabilities.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire ALL.SPACE for $355M
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring ALL.SPACE for $355 million to expand into high-growth markets like communications and military applications. The deal aims to combine technologies to enhance multi-domain capabilities.

title: Orbital Data Center Competitive Landscape
summary: The competitive landscape for orbital data centers includes major players like SpaceX, Blue Origin, Sophia Space, Starcloud, Aetherflux, and Axiom, as well as satellite manufacturing suppliers such as Apex and K2 Space.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: DPhi Space successfully demonstrated in-orbit AI processing by running a large language model on Liquid AI's satellite to analyze an Earth observation image without ground transmission. The demo, part of the Clustergate-2 mission launched on Momentus' Vigoride 7 in March, marks a significant advancement in space-based computing.

title: Golden Dome Will Use COTS Parts, Space Execs Say
summary: Apex Space CEO Ian Cinnamon stated that the Golden Dome satellite bus will use Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) parts, implying no unique advantages over existing commercial satellite buses.

title: Dems Slam Trump Over NSF Board Cuts
summary: Democrats criticized former President Trump for proposed cuts to the National Science Foundation (NSF) board, calling it detrimental to science and innovation. The statement was made by Rep. Zoe Lofgren (D-CA) in response to the policy change.

title: Loft to Build 10 EO Satellites for French Constellation With Magellium Artal Group
summary: Loft will build 10 Earth Observation (EO) satellites for a French constellation in partnership with Magellium Artal Group. This collaboration marks a significant step in France's next-generation EO satellite initiatives.

title: Orbital Warfare Systems Top Priority for Space Chief Nominee Lt. Gen. Schiess
summary: The White House's nominee to succeed Gen. B. Chance Saltzman prioritizes orbital warfare systems. Lt. Gen. Schiess Fielding emphasizes the need for more such systems.

title: Sateliot and Telefónica España to Explore 5G Satellite Connectivity for Defense, Military Uses
summary: Sateliot and Telefónica España have partnered to explore 5G satellite connectivity for defense and military applications, as well as expanding network coverage in remote areas.

title: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani Talks Network Resiliency at Modern Day Marine
summary: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani discussed the company's tactical, multi-orbit connectivity solutions at Modern Day Marine. The focus was on enhancing network resiliency for government applications.

title: Skynopy Signs Frequency Management Partnership with CNES
summary: Skynopy has partnered with CNES to optimize frequency allocation for its ground stations in France. The collaboration aims to enhance frequency management for Skynopy's operations.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Terminal Developer All.Space in $355M Deal
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring terminal developer All.Space in a $355 million deal to create a complete communications ecosystem. The acquisition aims to enhance York's capabilities in satellite communications.

title: FCC Updates EPFD Framework that Covers GEO/NGSO Spectrum Sharing
summary: The FCC has adopted a new framework for Equivalent Power Flux Density (EPFD) to govern spectrum-sharing between GEO and NGSO systems. This update addresses a key regulatory challenge in satellite communications.

title: EarthDaily CEO Don Osborne Previews Upcoming Launch in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: EarthDaily Analytics is preparing to launch six satellites this quarter, marking a significant milestone as the company transitions to commercial service. CEO Don Osborne discussed these upcoming developments in a Thursday morning conversation.

title: Department of Air Force Plans Initiative to Hire AI Talent
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force (DAF) announced an initiative to recruit top AI talent, which will impact the government's space strategy. This move highlights the growing intersection of AI and space sector priorities.

title: Canada Cancels WildFireSat Constellation Contract With Spire
summary: Canada has canceled its contract with Spire Global to build the WildFireSat constellation of wildfire-monitoring satellites. However, the government still intends to proceed with the space-based wildfire monitoring program.
